statement.markdown: Given a sorted array of integers `nums` and a target integer `target`, implement a binary search function that returns the index of the target if it is present in the array, or -1 otherwise. Your function should be case-insensitive and handle edge cases correctly.
statement.inputFormat: Input will be provided as an array of integers and a target integer.
statement.outputFormat: Output should be an integer representing the index of the target if found, or -1 otherwise.

examples.explanation: The binary search function should return the index of 3 in the array, which is 2.

hints.text: Consider the case where the target is the first element in the array.

editorial.title: Binary Search: Incorrect Early Return
editorial.intuition: The key to this problem is to implement a correct binary search algorithm that handles early returns correctly.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves implementing a binary search function that divides the search space in half at each step and returns the index of the target if found.
